title: Relapsed/refractory AML-MR with complex karyotype in a 71-year-old male

patient.chief_complaint: Fatigue, generalized weakness, exertional dyspnea, and persistent transfusion dependency
patient.vital_signs: BP 128/70 mmHg, HR 105/min, respiratory rate 22/min, T° 37.4 °C, oxygen saturation 95% on room air.

1.capabilities: You are evaluating an older patient with new or worsening symptoms in an ambulatory or day-unit setting. You can obtain a focused history and examination, request baseline laboratory evaluations, and arrange standard non-invasive imaging appropriate for this level of care. Invasive procedures and complex diagnostics usually require additional coordination and are not immediately available at the point of first contact.

2.capabilities: The patient is now admitted for further evaluation. You can organize invasive diagnostic procedures, a broader range of imaging, and initial pathology assessments. Interdisciplinary consultations and functional assessments can be requested as needed. Some specialized analyses may still be pending or require referral to external laboratories.

3.setting: Inpatient Ward / Transplant and Tumor Board Evaluation
3.capabilities: You now have access to results from specialized tests with longer turnaround times, including detailed cytogenetics, molecular profiling, and disease classification, as well as a complete longitudinal treatment history. You are expected to integrate these data with comorbidity assessments to define prognosis and formulate a definitive treatment strategy, including the role of allogeneic transplantation or palliative approaches.

demographics.valid_request_if: Requests for demographics or background characteristics such as age, sex, performance status, comorbidities, social history, or anthropometrics.
demographics.content:
Age: 71 years
Sex: Male
Performance status: ECOG 1
Comorbidities: Type 2 diabetes mellitus, hypertension, CKD stage 2, paroxysmal atrial fibrillation, prior prostate cancer treated with surgery, radiotherapy, and hormonal therapy
Social: Married; two healthy children.
Height: 176 cm; Body weight: 79 kg.

history_physical.valid_request_if: Requests for history of present illness, review of systems, physical examination findings, or current outpatient medication.
history_physical.content:
On August 5, 2024, the patient presented with fatigue, generalized weakness, and inability to exercise, accompanied by exertional dyspnea. Clinical signs of sarcopenia were present. He reported urinary incontinence following prior prostate cancer surgery (Gleason 7b). He had pneumonia three weeks earlier, which was treated with intravenous antibiotics at another hospital. Persistent transfusion-dependency (1/week).
Physical examination: Pale conjunctivae and reduced muscle mass consistent with sarcopenia. Mild exertional dyspnea observed on ambulation. No lymphadenopathy or hepatosplenomegaly. Cardiopulmonary examination otherwise unremarkable. Surgical scar in the pelvic region; mild urinary incontinence reported. No peripheral edema. Stable weight over the past month.
Current medication: Metformin 1,000 mg 1-0-1, empagliflozin 10 mg 1-0-0, ramipril 5 mg 1-0-0, amlodipine 5 mg 1-0-0, bisoprolol 5 mg 1-0-1, apixaban 2.5 mg 1-0-0, atorvastatin 20 mg 1-0-0.

cbc.valid_request_if: Requests for a complete blood count, including hemoglobin, white blood cell count, platelets, absolute neutrophil count, and differential.
cbc.content: Hb 8.1 g/dL, WBC 1.5 ×10⁹/L, Plt 18 ×10⁹/L; ANC 0.6 ×10⁹/L, lymphocytes 0.7 ×10⁹/L, monocytes 0.2 ×10⁹/L; no peripheral blasts.

coagulation.valid_request_if: Requests for coagulation parameters such as INR or aPTT.

chemistry.valid_request_if: Requests for a chemistry or metabolic panel, including renal and hepatic function tests and uric acid.
chemistry.content: Na 136 mmol/L, K 3.3 mmol/L, creatinine 1.6 mg/dL (eGFR 55 mL/min/1.73 m²), AST 75 U/L, ALT 73 U/L, bilirubin 1.4 mg/dL, LDH 325 U/L, uric acid 7.1 mg/dL.

ultrasound_abdomen.valid_request_if: Requests for abdominal ultrasound or sonography of abdominal organs.
ultrasound_abdomen.content: August 6, 2024: Abdominal ultrasound: Mild splenomegaly (14.5 cm) and hepatic steatosis with smooth liver margins and no focal lesions. The gallbladder appeared unremarkable, and intrahepatic bile ducts were not dilated. Postoperative changes consistent with prior prostate cancer surgery were noted in the pelvic region. The right kidney showed normal size and parenchymal structure, while the left kidney appeared shrunken (8.1 cm) with reduced cortical thickness and preserved perfusion. No ascites or retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy detected.

ct_thorax_abdomen.valid_request_if: Requests for contrast-enhanced CT of the thorax and abdomen or similar cross-sectional imaging.
ct_thorax_abdomen.content: CT scan (August 6, 2024): Thoracic and abdominal contrast-enhanced CT demonstrated residual linear scarring in the right lower lobe compatible with prior pneumonia. A solitary pulmonary nodule (1.2 cm) with peripheral ground-glass halo in the right lower lobe was identified. No pleural effusion or mediastinal lymphadenopathy was seen. Mild splenomegaly and hepatic steatosis were confirmed; no focal hepatic or osseous lesions detected.

echocardiography.valid_request_if: Requests for transthoracic echocardiography or cardiac ultrasound.
echocardiography.content: Echocardiogram (August 7, 2024): Atrial fibrillation (104 bpm) with a globally mildly reduced left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF 49%). Mild left atrial enlargement and grade I diastolic dysfunction were noted. No significant valvular pathology or pericardial effusion observed.

bone_marrow.valid_request_if: Requests for bone marrow aspirate or biopsy morphology findings.
bone_marrow.content: Bone marrow (July 28, 2024): Morphology revealed hypocellular bone marrow with 25–30% myeloblasts. Dysplastic changes were noted across myeloid lineages, characterized by toxic granulation and hypolobulated nuclei. Erythropoiesis and granulopoiesis were markedly reduced; megakaryocytes were decreased and dysplastic.

bone_marrow_flow.valid_request_if: Requests for flow cytometry or immunophenotyping of the bone marrow.
bone_marrow_flow.content: Flow cytometry: Blasts (26% of total nucleated cells) expressed CD34, CD117, HLA-DR, CD13, CD33, and CD38. Partial expression of CD15 and weak MPO positivity were observed. Aberrant co-expression of CD5 and CD7 was noted. Negative for lymphoid markers (CD19, CD20, CD10, CD79a, CD3, TdT). Maturing myeloid and monocytic populations were markedly reduced, with preserved but quantitatively decreased erythroid precursors.

cytogenetics.valid_request_if: Requests for conventional cytogenetic analysis or karyotype.

infectious_workup.valid_request_if: Requests for infection screening, inflammatory markers, microbiology, or colonization status.
infectious_workup.content: Infection screen: CRP 15.2 mg/L. Blood cultures negative. Serum galactomannan 0.92; β-D-glucan not performed. CMV PCR negative. HSV and EBV PCRs negative. Microbiology: Nasopharyngeal swab negative for influenza, RSV, and SARS-CoV-2. Rectal swab showed colonization with ESBL-producing E. coli with metallo-β-lactamase (VIM). MRSA negative, VRE negative.

cardiopulmonary_assessment.valid_request_if: Requests for cardiology or pulmonary function assessments beyond the initial echocardiogram.
cardiopulmonary_assessment.content:
Cardiology: ECG demonstrated atrial fibrillation with a ventricular rate of 121/min and T-wave inversions in leads V3–V6.
Pulmonary function testing: Reduced diffusion capacity (DLCO 50%) and reduced FEV1 (65%), consistent with impaired pulmonary reserve.

prostate_status.valid_request_if: Requests for evaluation of prior prostate cancer status or pelvic imaging.
prostate_status.content: Pelvic ultrasound: No residual or recurrent soft tissue mass. PSA: <0.03 ng/mL, Testosterone: 3.9 ng/mL.

molecular_panel.valid_request_if: Requests for molecular profiling, next-generation sequencing, or mutation panel results.
molecular_panel.content: Mutations in DDX41 (VAF 43%), ASXL1 (VAF 27%), RUNX1 (VAF 34%), TET2 (VAF 41%), and PTPN11 (VAF 11%). No mutations in NPM1, FLT3-ITD/TKD, IDH1/2, CEBPA, or TP53. No KMT2A rearrangement detected.

mrd_status.valid_request_if: Requests for measurable residual disease characterization, including leukemia-associated immunophenotype or molecular markers.
mrd_status.content: AML with DDX41, ASXL1, RUNX1, TET2 and PTPN11 mutations. Leukemia-associated immunophenotype (LAIP): CD34, CD117, HLA-DR, CD13, CD33, CD38, CD15 positive and weak MPO.

treatment_history.valid_request_if: Requests for prior AML treatments, transplant plans, maintenance therapy, and relapse course.
treatment_history.content:
2022-08-12: Initial diagnosis of AML with myelodysplasia-related changes (AML-MR).
2022-08-18: Induction I with CPX-351 (liposomal: daunorubicin 44 mg/m² + cytarabine 100 mg/m², 3 days). Complications: Neutropenic fever and pneumonia. Acute kidney injury (creatinine 2.4 mg/dL). The patient received piperacillin/tazobactam q8h for 5 days, then dose-reduced to 4.5 g q12h due to decreased GFR, and later switched to meropenem 1 g q12h after recurrence of fever. Posaconazole 300 mg/d was started on day 5, but switched to liposomal amphotericin B (300 mg/d) after CT scan (2022-08-24) revealed suspicious pulmonary nodules. Mucositis treated with hydromorphone continuous intravenous infusion (max dose 0.8 mg/h). The patient remained transfusion-dependent due to cytopenia. Hematopoietic recovery: 2022-10-02, with leukocytes >1,000/µL and platelets >20 ×10⁹/L. Result: Partial remission (PR) with a reduction of bone marrow blasts to 8%. The patient was switched back from liposomal amphotericin B to posaconazole. A matched unrelated donor (MUD) was identified.
2022-10-16: Induction II with CPX-351 (daunorubicin 44 mg/m² + cytarabine 100 mg/m², 2 days). Complications: Pneumonia with neutropenic fever. CT scan (2022-10-27) showed atypical pneumonia with bilateral ground-glass opacities and a halo sign suggestive of invasive fungal pneumonia. Liposomal amphotericin B 300 mg/d was initiated, and isavuconazole 200 mg TID for 2 days followed by 200 mg/d was added after repeat CT (2022-11-05) showed progression. Prolonged thrombocytopenia with minor epistaxis occurred. Hematopoietic recovery: 2022-11-28. Result (2022-11-30): Complete remission (CR) with 2% bone marrow blasts.
2022-12-12: Consolidation I with CPX-351. Result (2023-01-23): Ongoing CR. Planned allogeneic transplantation postponed due to incidental finding of prostate carcinoma during routine check-up.
2023-02-20: Consolidation II with CPX-351. Result (2023-03-23): CR.
2023-05-01: Radical prostatectomy for prostate cancer (Gleason 7b). Postoperative complication: Urinary tract infection treated with ceftriaxone 2 g/d for 7 days.
2023-05-12: Remission control: CR, no evidence of MRD (flow cytometry and NGS negative). Start of maintenance therapy with oral azacitidine (Onureg) 300 mg once daily, days 1–14 of each 28-day cycle.
2023-11-21: 1% aberrant blasts detected by flow cytometry. Onureg therapy continued with 200 mg days 1–14 of each 28-day cycle.
2024-05-01: Hematologic relapse with 39% blasts. DDX41 mutation again detectable; ASXL1, RUNX1, TET2, and PTPN11 also present. Complex karyotype identified.
2024-05-08: Start of 1st cycle azacitidine (75 mg/m², 7 days, total 1,036 mg) + venetoclax days 1–14 (200 mg/d, total dose 2,800 mg). Complications: Severe neutropenia, no G-CSF support due to residual disease, febrile episode, no pathogen isolated. Result: 32% blasts by flow cytometry and cytology.
2024-06-15: Cycle 2 initiated. Bone marrow (response): 33% blasts by flow cytometry and cytology. Continued with cycle 3 in July 2024.

disease_status.valid_request_if: Requests for a concise summary of the current leukemia status or relapse classification.
disease_status.content: Relapse type: Relapsed/refractory AML with myelodysplasia-related changes (AML-MR) with complex karyotype and persistent high blast burden despite azacitidine and venetoclax.

special_considerations.valid_request_if: Requests for special considerations relevant to treatment planning, including comorbidity scores, organ dysfunction, infection status, and patient preferences.
special_considerations.content: HCT-CI score: 6, due to cardiac dysfunction, pulmonary impairment, active infection, and history of prior solid tumor. AML is not in remission; the patient currently has an active pulmonary lesion suspicious for fungal pneumonia despite ongoing isavuconazole treatment. Serum galactomannan is borderline positive. Echocardiography reveals permanent atrial fibrillation with globally mildly reduced ejection fraction of approximately 50%. Despite his age (71 years) and substantial comorbidity burden, the patient remains motivated and wishes to pursue all available treatment options for AML. The disease appears to be venetoclax-refractory, and prior exposure to intensive chemotherapy and maintenance hypomethylating agents limits further conventional options.

treatment_plan.action: Obtain broad infection cultures (blood, urine, swabs)
Platelet transfusion before bone marrow biopsy
Pulmonary function test for exertional dyspnea

key_findings_quotes: Fatigue, generalized weakness, and inability to exercise, accompanied by exertional dyspnea
Prostate cancer treated with surgery, radiotherapy and hormonal therapy
Globally mildly reduced left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF 49%) and atrial fibrillation (104 bpm)

treatment_plan.action: Perform full molecular panel (FLT3, NPM1, IDH, CEBPA)
Start antibiotics adjusted for ESBL-producing E. coli with metallo-β-lactamase (VIM)
Isolation due to colonization with VIM-producing E. coli

key_findings_quotes: Bone marrow: Morphology revealed hypocellular bone marrow with 25–30% myeloblasts
Dysplastic changes were noted across myeloid lineages, characterized by toxic granulation and hypolobulated nuclei
Blasts (26% of total nucleated cells) expressed CD34, CD117, HLA-DR, CD13, CD33, and CD38
Aberrant co-expression of CD5 and CD7 was noted. Negative for lymphoid markers (CD19, CD20, CD10, CD79a, CD3, TdT)
Rectal swab showed colonization with ESBL-producing E. coli with metallo-β-lactamase (VIM)
Reduced diffusion capacity (DLCO 50%) and reduced FEV1 (65%), consistent with impaired pulmonary reserve

round_3.working_diagnosis: Relapsed/refractory AML with myelodysplasia-related changes and complex karyotype, ELN 2022 risk: Adverse
differentials: Active invasive fungal pneumonia complicating treatment

treatment_plan.action: Treat active fungal infection with liposomal amphotericin B 5-10 mg/kg/d
Allogeneic SCT with FLAMSA-RIC sequential conditioning from matched unrelated donor as only curative option
GVHD prophylaxis with cyclosporine A and mycophenolate mofetil
Re-evaluate allo-SCT feasibility given HCT-CI Score 6 and AML not in remission
Continue infection prophylaxis and transfusion support
Consider compassionate use program for new antifungals (olorofim or fosmanogepix) if fungal infection worsens

key_findings_quotes: Mutations in DDX41 (VAF 43%), ASXL1 (VAF 27%), RUNX1 (VAF 34%), TET2 (VAF 41%), and PTPN11 (VAF 11%)
No mutations in NPM1, FLT3-ITD/TKD, IDH1/2, CEBPA, or TP53
Hematologic relapse with 39% blasts. DDX41 mutation again detectable; ASXL1, RUNX1, TET2, and PTPN11 also present. Complex karyotype identified
Result: 32% blasts by flow cytometry and cytology after azacitidine + venetoclax
HCT-CI score: 6, due to cardiac dysfunction, pulmonary impairment, active infection, and history of prior solid tumor
Active pulmonary lesion suspicious for fungal pneumonia despite ongoing isavuconazole treatment
